title: Lobster Mock BFF API Schema Bundle

conversationList.summary: Future endpoint for fetching conversation list for the desktop shell.

conversations.name: Otaku Budget Group
conversations.preview: Aman: Want to visit the merch stores this weekend?

conversationMessages.summary: Future endpoint for fetching message flow for a selected conversation.

messages.content: Anyone wants to split shipping for the maid acrylic stand set?

detectIntent.summary: Future endpoint for classifying whether a message contains a Lobster-worthy social signal.

requestBody.messageText: Anyone wants to watch Dune 2 together this weekend?
contextWindow: I kind of want to rewatch it.

intent.reasoningSummary: Detected a coordination signal for shared viewing.

generateCard.summary: Future endpoint for generating a structured Lobster card using rules plus Aliyun LLM.

requestBody.userPrompt: Create a weekend watch party card for Dune 2.
contextWindow: People in the group have been talking about Dune 2.
Several fictional personas want to find a time to go together.

card.title: Weekend Dune 2 Watch Party
card.description: Two people look interested. Want to make it official?

cardAction.summary: Future endpoint for applying a deterministic state transition to an existing card.

implementationNotes: Current phase can run entirely from local JSON and in-memory state without any HTTP layer.
The API bundle here documents the future local BFF contract once the project outgrows the static narrative phase.
All card-generating endpoints should validate structured outputs against domain schema before returning to the client.
Aliyun LLM should be called only by the BFF adapter layer.
